source_jobs.title: Fundraising Copywriter
source_jobs.description:
Hi, I'm Jade, the People & Culture Lead at ntegrity.

I get to spend my days with a team https://ntegrity.com.au/about-us/ that genuinely lives our values, partnering with organisations like Beyond Blue, The Salvation Army, and The Fred Hollows Foundation to drive real social impact. Our B Corp certification is a commitment to doing business the right way, not just getting results. That means investing in our people's growth, exploring new ways of working, and treating culture as our operating system, where the bar is high because the mission actually matters.

Choosing a new role is a significant decision, and we are delighted you would consider us. Below you'll find an honest picture of the role: what it involves, what it takes to thrive here, and the honest signs it might not be the right fit. If you look like a strong match, we'll be in touch with next steps, including detailed information about the interview process and salary.

About the role

ntegrity is a full-service agency with 95% not-for-profit clients. Our mission is to "help good grow." Our clients look to us for direction on how to grow their results, grow their team's capabilities, and in turn, grow their impact on the world.

We're looking for a Fundraising Copywriter to join a cross-functional squad. You'll own the copywriting function across digital and traditional channels, working closely with strategists and designers from brief through to delivery. That means capturing attention, evoking emotion, and directly driving fundraising revenue for some of Australia's most respected organisations.

About you:

 - Experience: 6-10 years of copywriting experience with a working command of how your craft drives income across the fundraising funnel (acquisition, conversion, retention, reactivation).

 - Sector & Environment: Proven track record working within both a fast-paced agency environment and the not-for-profit sector, with a clear understanding of the unique communication nuances and strategic challenges of fundraising.

 - Autonomy & Performance: Strong organisational skills with a proven ability to meet targets through solid self-management, ensuring on-time client delivery without needing manager reminders.

 - Tech & AI Fluency: Confident, responsible use of AI tools (like Claude and Gemini) to improve speed and efficiency, using AI outputs as inputs for thinking rather than replacements for human strategy.

 - Growth Mindset: Actively seeks out constructive feedback, integrates it into individual development plans, and remains open to critique without becoming defensive.

 - Resilience: Able to identify personal and professional stressors and take proactive action to manage them, limiting the impact on co-workers.

What You Will Be Doing:

 - Concepting & Writing: Designing concepts into creative briefs and executing high-quality, persuasive copy across headlines, emails, social posts, landing pages, and direct mail with limited review needed.

 - Data-Driven Tactics: Co-designing tactics (lead capture, warm-up pathways, reactivation) and using data (ROI, CPA, retention rates) to explain creative performance in plain language.

 - Project Ownership: Stepping into a project lead role on small projects (1-3 team members) from inception to execution and managing timelines.

 - Client Leadership: Probing to understand the root cause of client problems, recapping pain points without craft jargon, and presenting confidently to Senior Managers.

This Role Won’t be a Good Fit If:

 - You require daily oversight: If you need a daily checkpoint, constant hand-holding, or close supervision to remain detail-oriented, this level of autonomy will feel overwhelming.

 - You neglect financial metrics: If you cannot tie your creative work back to hard fundraising metrics (CPA, ROI), or if you struggle to track your time daily to hit billable targets.

 - You copy-paste AI outputs: If you use public generative AI tools without verifying outputs for accuracy, bias, and or

source_jobs.title: Senior Creative Designer
source_jobs.description:
Hi, I'm Jade, the People & Culture Lead at ntegrity.

I get to spend my days with a team that genuinely lives our values, partnering with organisations like Beyond Blue, The Salvation Army, and The Fred Hollows Foundation to drive real social impact. Our B Corp certification is a commitment to doing business the right way, not just getting results. That means investing in our people's growth, exploring new ways of working, and treating culture as our operating system, where the bar is high because the mission actually matters.

Choosing a new role is a significant decision, and we are delighted you would consider us. Below you'll find an honest picture of the role: what it involves, what it takes to thrive here, and the honest signs it might not be the right fit. If you look like a strong match, we'll be in touch with next steps, including detailed information about the interview process and salary.


About the Role

ntegrity is a full-service agency with 95% not-for-profit clients. Our mission is to "help good grow." Our clients look to us for direction on how to grow their results, grow their team's capabilities, and in turn, grow their impact on the world.

We're looking for a Senior Creative Designer to join a cross-functional squad. You'll lead the visual elements of our work from initial ideation to final delivery, acting as both the creative lead and the strategic anchor for our projects and clients. You'll bring a deep understanding of fundraising principles, design, art direction, and advertising effectiveness to drive visual aesthetics across the full range of campaigns and marketing channels.

As a natural leader, you will confidently steer end-to-end creative projects, collaborate directly with key clients to understand their vision, and translate complex briefs into high-impact visual strategies. Your creative output will always be anchored to performance: every design decision connects back to fundraising outcomes, donor response, and measurable campaign results.


About You

 - Experience: 10+ years of design experience, with at least 3-5 years in a fundraising or performance agency environment. Agency background is essential, you understand how to work within cross-functional squads, manage competing client priorities, and deliver under real commercial pressure.

 - Fundraising Knowledge: A strong working knowledge of how design and creative ladders to fundraising outcomes. You understand acquisition, retention, and donor lifetime value, and you bring that lens to every brief.

 - Performance-Driven Creative: Proven experience designing creative that is built to perform, not just to look good. You are comfortable working with campaign data, testing hypotheses visually, and iterating based on results. You know the difference between a brand campaign and a fundraising campaign, and you design accordingly.

 - Project Leadership: Proven ability to lead complex, multi-stream projects end-to-end, setting objectives, aligning teams, managing scope, and keeping delivery on track without needing close oversight.

 - Creative Excellence & Art Direction: An outstanding portfolio of category-disrupting campaigns across digital, video, print, and direct mail, with results to back them up. Deep expertise in visual storytelling, art direction, and multi-channel design, with a platform-first approach to content creation.

 - Client Presence: Confident presenting to senior stakeholders including CMOs and Heads of Marketing. You can translate complex creative thinking into plain language, navigate pushback, and guide clients to informed decisions.

 - Leadership & Feedback: Bold creative confidence with the ability to give and receive direct, constructive feedback. You bring others along rather than gatekeeping the work.

 - Tools & Delivery: Proficiency across the Adobe Creative Suite and relevant design tools. Confident, responsible use of AI tools to improve creative speed and quality, using AI outputs as inputs for thinking

source_jobs.title: Join Our Freelance Network!
source_jobs.description:
ntegrity is a full-service agency with 95% not-for-profit clients. Our mission is to “help good grow”. Our clients look to us to give them direction on how to grow their results, grow their team's capabilities and in turn, grow their impact on the world.

We're are seeking freelancers that have experience working with not-for-profit and/or for purpose organisations. This is an ongoing job opening



Who We're Looking For:

 - Content Creation: Writers, editors, copywriters, content strategists, proofreaders.

 - Design & Creative: Graphic designers, web designers, UI/UX designers.

 - Digital Marketing: SEO/SEM specialists, social media managers, email marketers, marketing strategists, data analysts.

 - And more! If you have a unique skill set, we're interested in learning about it.

What We're Looking For in You:

 - Proven Experience: A strong portfolio showcasing your skills and expertise working with not-for-profit and/or for purpose organisations.

 - Excellent Communication: Clear and professional communication skills.

 - Reliability & Professionalism: Ability to meet deadlines and deliver high-quality work.

 - Self-Motivation: Ability to work independently and manage your own time effectively.

 - Adaptability: Willingness to learn and adapt to new projects and challenges.

 - A passion for your work!

If you are interested in potential collaboration opportunities please submit the following:

 - Your resume/CV.

 - A link to your portfolio or samples of your work.

 - Your preferred rate or rate range.

We look forward to connecting with you and exploring potential collaboration opportunities!

source_jobs.title: SEO Performance Manager (12 month contract)
source_jobs.description:
Hi, I'm Jade, the People & Culture Lead at ntegrity.

I get to spend my days with a team that genuinely lives our values, partnering with organisations like Beyond Blue, The Salvation Army, and The Fred Hollows Foundation to drive real social impact. Our B Corp certification is a commitment to doing business the right way, not just getting results. That means investing in our people's growth, exploring new ways of working, and treating culture as our operating system, where the bar is high because the mission actually matters.

Choosing a new role is a significant decision, and we are delighted you would consider us. Below you'll find an honest picture of the role: what it involves, what it takes to thrive here, and the honest signs it might not be the right fit. If you look like a strong match, we'll be in touch with next steps, including detailed information about the interview process and salary.


About the Role

ntegrity is a full-service agency with 95% not-for-profit clients. Our mission is to "help good grow." Our clients look to us for direction on how to grow their results, grow their team's capabilities, and in turn, grow their impact on the world.

We're looking for an SEO Performance Manager to join our team. You'll own the strategy, execution, and reporting of SEO across a portfolio of purpose-driven clients, acting as the bridge between data and the client. This isn't a task-runner role. You'll be expected to think strategically, identify what the numbers are actually saying, and translate that into clear recommendations clients can act on.

You'll work closely with cross-functional squads across content, digital performance, and client success, presenting findings directly to clients and contributing to the organic growth of organisations doing meaningful work.

This role is fully remote, based anywhere in Malaysia or The Philippines, working Australian Eastern Time (AET) with client meetings held virtually. It will begin as a contract position with the potential for ongoing employment after 12 months.


About You

 - Experience: 5-7 years of dedicated SEO experience, ideally within a digital agency or freelance environment. You'll be managing dashboards across 11 client accounts from day one, so prior agency experience is required over in-house only.

 - Technical SEO: Strong working knowledge of site health auditing, crawlability, indexation, and site speed. Comfortable identifying and resolving issues across complex websites including subdomains, heavy JavaScript use, and localisation.

 - SEO Tools: Advanced proficiency in Ahrefs. Familiarity with Semrush, Lumar, and Screaming Frog is a strong advantage.

 - Data & Reporting: Proven experience building, maintaining, and troubleshooting Looker Studio dashboards. Strong command of GA4 and Google Search Console to stitch together a complete performance picture.

 - Content Strategy: Ability to conduct in-depth keyword research and translate findings into structured, SEO and GEO-driven content outlines that creative teams and clients can act on.

 - Communication & Storytelling: Exceptional written and verbal English. You can look at a chart, explain what happened, and outline what comes next, without relying on technical jargon. Clients should leave every conversation clearer, not more confused.

 - Analytical Mindset: Naturally curious about traffic fluctuations and algorithm shifts. You ask why before you ask what to fix.

 - Bonus: Familiarity with CMS platforms (WordPress, Shopify, or Webflow), Google Tag Manager, programmatic display, or conversion rate optimisation.


What You Will Be Doing

 - Dashboard Management: Own and manage up to 10 automated Looker Studio dashboards, tracking performance month-on-month and translating raw data into high-value insights, not just numbers on a slide.

 - Technical Auditing: Conduct comprehensive monthly technical health audits using Ahrefs and supplementary tools, identifying and prioritising crawl errors, indexatio

source_jobs.title: Digital Performance Manager (12 month contract)
source_jobs.description:
Hi, I'm Jade, the People & Culture Lead at ntegrity.

I get to spend my days with a team that genuinely lives our values, partnering with organisations like Beyond Blue, The Salvation Army, and The Fred Hollows Foundation to drive real social impact. Our B Corp certification is a commitment to doing business the right way, not just getting results. That means investing in our people's growth, exploring new ways of working, and treating culture as our operating system, where the bar is high because the mission actually matters.

Choosing a new role is a significant decision, and we are delighted you would consider us. Below you'll find an honest picture of the role: what it involves, what it takes to thrive here, and the honest signs it might not be the right fit. If you look like a strong match, we'll be in touch with next steps, including detailed information about the interview process and salary.


About the Role

ntegrity is a full-service agency with 95% not-for-profit clients. Our mission is to "help good grow." Our clients look to us for direction on how to grow their results, grow their team's capabilities, and in turn, grow their impact on the world.

We're looking for a Digital Performance Manager to join our performance team. You'll lead the planning, optimisation, and analysis of paid media campaigns across Google, Meta, and other key platforms, translating data into clear recommendations that deliver measurable outcomes for purpose-driven clients. You'll work closely with clients and internal teams, acting as a trusted advisor who connects digital performance to real organisational growth.

This role is fully remote, based anywhere in Malaysia or The Philippines, working Australian Eastern Time (AET) with client meetings held virtually. It will begin as a contract position with the potential for ongoing employment after 12 months.


About You

 - Experience: 3-5 years managing digital marketing campaigns in an agency or client-side environment.

 - Platform Expertise: Proven command of Google Ads and Meta Ads, including campaign setup, optimisation, and reporting.

 - Communication: Excellent written and verbal English, with strong attention to detail and the ability to present insights confidently to clients.

 - Analytical Confidence: Experience with performance tracking, KPI reporting, and tools such as Looker Studio or Google Analytics.

 - Bonus: Familiarity with programmatic display advertising.


What You Will Be Doing

 - Campaign Management: Plan and deliver high-performing campaigns across Google, Meta, and other channels, ensuring every activity meets defined KPIs and delivers measurable ROI.

 - Insight Generation: Analyse performance data to uncover trends and opportunities, translating findings into actionable strategy in plain language.

 - Client Leadership: Build trusted client partnerships through proactive communication, clear recommendations, and a genuine understanding of their goals.

 - Execution Standards: Uphold best-practice standards across budgets, tracking, and delivery to ensure accuracy and consistency.

 - Continuous Improvement: Stay ahead of digital trends, test new tools, and contribute to team learning and capability growth.


This Role Won't Be a Good Fit If

 - You prefer routine over variety: This role requires adaptability, initiative, and comfort with shifting priorities.

 - You rely on others to set strategy: We're looking for someone who takes ownership and drives performance without waiting to be directed.

 - You're uncomfortable with clients: Presenting insights and recommendations directly to clients is a core part of this role.

 - You avoid the detail: Accuracy, reporting, and proactive optimisation are critical to success here.

 - You're looking for a short-term opportunity: We want someone invested in growing with us, not just filling a remote role. We love flexibility and trust our people completely, but we hold high expectations for commitment and output.


Wh

notes: Non-tech org with a few non-tech roles, no explicit mention of sponsorship or clearance
